goal: Finish the Capsem 1.3 release correction to a fully working, tested, and trusted release: close the remaining Ironbank black-box ledger gaps, prove model/provider/MCP/DNS/HTTP/file/process/credential paths end to end, finish UI and TUI route contract wiring, verify install/package/profile assets, and pass the full release gate without fallback paths or compatibility escape hatches.
context_notes: The old markdown trackers remain historical context only; Sprinty is the source of truth from this point forward.
Realm discipline: no fallback, no compatibility shape, no duplicate engines, no fast/skip/dry-run/check escape hatches. One path, exact contract, tests prove it.

Ironbank discipline: black-box tests in tests/ironbank/ prove full ledger accounting across client result, routes, DB rows, logs, security decision, detection/enforcement/plugin rows, protocol rows, and UI JSON. What goes in must come out exactly.
Recent completed commit f2377107: compact pricing ledger transformed from pydantic/genai-prices prices/data.json, strict upstream match only, no fuzzy pricing fallback.

Known remaining debts include real/recorded provider tests for OpenAI Responses/messages, Gemini, Claude, AGY; hermetic mock-server replay; MCP unknown discovery and tool/list/call ledger proof; DNS/HTTP/file/process route proofs; credential broker capture/injection/rewrite proof; UI profile/routes display fixes; install/package manifest gate; final just test, just install, doctor, benchmark, and CI gate.

description: S1 Ironbank protocol ledger: build and run black-box tests that exercise HTTP, HTTPS/SSE, DNS, MCP list/tool call/tool response, file events, process events, credential broker capture/injection/rewrite, security decisions, detection/enforcement/plugin rows, structured logs, UDS/HTTP route responses, and UI-facing JSON with exact field assertions.
goals: Use tests/ironbank as the release ledger suite, not Rust parser tests.
One deterministic stimulus per protocol asserts client-visible result plus every relevant DB/log/route/UI field.
No skip/slow/fast/public-network paths; all upstreams use scripts/mock_server_runtime.py.
Credential broker proof includes captured, injected, and brokered events with keychain/store behavior mocked only at public boundary if needed.
MCP proof includes unknown MCP discovery from network/process signals and exact tool list/call/response cardinality.

description: S2 Provider/model proof: prove OpenAI Responses and messages, Gemini, Claude API/SDK/CLI, Codex CLI, AGY, and Ollama-shaped local provider traffic with recorded live fixtures and hermetic replay through the Capsem path, including two-turn dedupe, tool calls, tool responses, thinking/content split, token counts, bytes, pricing, and credential brokage.
goals: Live optional provider tests use .env keys only when present, never committed, and bake equivalent hermetic replay fixtures for release proof.
OpenAI covers Responses API, messages/chat shape, embeddings, and image/vision path where supported by configured models.
Gemini covers configured Gemini model constants and image generation where supported.
Claude covers API, SDK, and real CLI invocation; Codex covers real CLI subprocess writing random file contents; AGY is isolated after shared provider rails pass.
Two-turn tests assert exact cardinality by trace/response id: requests, responses, reasoning, tool calls, tool responses, HTTP, DNS, file events, security rows, and pricing rows.

description: Ironbank HTTP protocol ledger matrix: plain JSON allow, denied, ask, broker rewrite, gzip/chunked/truncated/SSE/HTTPS cases each assert client result, upstream transcript, net_events, security_rule_events, substitution rows where relevant, structured logs, UDS inspect, gateway inspect, status counters, and UI-facing serialization fields.

Ironbank DNS ledger matrix: UDP DNS query/response and blocked/exfiltration cases assert qname/qtype/qclass/rcode/ip.value/udp facts, resolver timing, security decisions, detection rows, DNS counters, structured logs, UDS/gateway latest output, and no bypass of HTTP allowlists via arbitrary DNS.

Ironbank MCP ledger matrix: native profile MCP server/tool discovery, unknown remote MCP detection, tools/list, tools/call, tool response, tool arguments, route-visible server/tool state, CEL decisions, mcp_calls/tool_calls/tool_responses/security rows, and no phantom snapshot/tool calls.

Ironbank file/process/snapshot ledger matrix: file create/modify/delete/import/export rows, process audit versus exec semantics, package-manager functional probes, snapshot route hermeticity, symlink escape prevention, compact summaries, trace linkage, and UI-facing table fields all match the DB ledger.

gates.spec: rg -n "snapshot.*db|snapshot.*activity|skip|slow|presence only|installed only" tests/ironbank guest/artifacts/diagnostics && false || true

description: Ironbank credential broker and plugin ledger matrix: captured/injected/brokered credential events, plugin pre/post/logging stages, plugin enable/disable/mode/detection-level route effects, keychain/store readiness/retry status, raw-secret absence, structured logs, counters, and DB rows all agree.

gates.spec: rg -n "OPENAI_API_KEY|GOOGLE_API_KEY|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|sk-|xox|raw_secret" test-artifacts tests/ironbank crates/capsem-logger && false || true

Single security rail burn-down: remove and forbid old/parallel decision engines including policy_v2 hooks, domain policy hooks, NetworkPolicy blocking decisions, McpPolicy/LocalMcpDecisionProvider, callback-string decision providers, legacy_decision, policy.http.*, policy.mcp.*, policy.model.*, fallback logger paths, and side DB writes. Every live HTTP, DNS, MCP, model, file, and process boundary must emit a typed SecurityEvent, evaluate SecurityRuleSet only, append detections/enforcements/plugins into the same ledger, and write through the single DbWriter.

gates.spec: rg -n "policy_v2|PolicyV2|NetworkPolicy|McpPolicy|LocalMcpDecisionProvider|legacy_decision|policy\.http|policy\.mcp|policy\.model|callback.*decision|fallback logger|fallback_logger|write_many|side write" crates tests && false || true

description: Plugin stage contract and performance proof: plugins are declared as typed pre, post, or logging stages; each plugin receives a SecurityEvent and emits a SecurityEvent; plugins may mutate decisions/detections before logging but cannot create a second decision engine; block decisions are absolute; dummy_pre, dummy_post, dummy_logging, credential_broker, and log_sanitizer exercise enable/disable/mode/detection_level/rewrite behavior; every plugin has name, description, version, stage, counters, latency histograms, profile/corp config, route status, and docs page metadata.

gates.spec: rg -n "plugin.*decision engine|plugin.*fallback|security plugin|dummy_(pre|post|logging)|credential_broker|log_sanitizer|stage" crates config tests docs site

description: Credential store and broker lifecycle contract: credential access goes through one CredentialStore object backed by macOS Keychain service org.capsem.credentials and a documented Linux fallback until the Linux secure-store issue is solved; service startup loads credentials into protected memory once, distinguishes empty from degraded, exposes retry/status/info routes, avoids repeated Keychain prompts on hot paths, records captured/injected/brokered security events, and uses the single DbWriter for all persistence-visible facts.

CEL fact model and default-rule invariant contract: rule evaluation exposes only first-party typed event roots that exist in the SecurityEvent contract: http, dns, mcp, model, file, process, ip, tcp, udp, and plugin-produced fields; credential/snapshot roots are forbidden unless backed by real event producers. Provider and protocol are enums, not aliases. Rules support action allow/ask/block/rewrite/preprocess/postprocess/disable, detection_level none/informational/low/medium/high/critical, default priority sugar, built-in max+1 priority, corp negative priority, and local/private network ask-by-default plus Ollama allow toggle through normal rules. Unknown model provider and unknown MCP server detections are default rules with exact CEL tests.

gates.spec: rg -n "credential\.|snapshot\.|is_private\(|is_loopback\(|provider.*alias|aliases\s*=|policy\.|priority.*default|unknown_provider|unknown_mcp|local.*ask" crates config tests && false || true

text: Gate clarification: split the grep gate when implementing. Forbidden terms are credential.*, snapshot.*, is_private(), is_loopback(), provider aliases, old policy.*. Required terms are priority default, unknown_provider, unknown_mcp, local ask/default network rules, and explicit ip/tcp/udp/model/mcp facts. Do not leave the current combined grep as a release gate.
